item.title: Brazil Police Target Lula Ally in Banco Master Probe
item.description: Brazil’s federal police targeted Senator Jaques Wagner, the government leader in the upper house, as part of an investigation into alleged irregularities linked to Banco Master, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Warsh Establishes Inflation Credibility Now, Sets Up Fed Regime Change Later
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh vowed to restore price stability after leaving rates unchanged.

item.title: Lawyer Takes Fight to Keep Kids Off Social Media From Courthouse to Political Arena
item.description: Matthew Bergman pitched UK officials to protect kids from social media harms

item.title: Bank of England Holds Rates in 7-2 Vote as Oil Outlook Improves
item.description: The Bank of England held interest rates at 3.75% as it said the recent fall in oil prices was “encouraging,” even while two policymakers voted for an immediate quarter-point hike over concerns of persistent inflation.

item.title: Big Tech’s AI Spending Spree Is Making Stock Buybacks Disappear
item.description: The artificial-intelligence race is becoming so expensive that it’s snuffing out one of the key forces that has helped keep Big Tech stocks soaring for years: steady share buybacks.

item.title: Ships Race to Scrub Barnacles After Months Stuck in Persian Gulf
item.description: Vessels are getting scrubbed as they prepare to starting hauling cargo again

item.title: Europe Scales Down AI Ambitions With Smaller Data Center Tender
item.description: The European Union downsized plans for data centers to support artificial intelligence, outlining a call for tenders that includes smaller-scale facilities than originally envisioned.

item.title: US Treasuries Rebound After Warsh’s Debut at Fed Stoked Selloff
item.description: US Treasuries recovered from a selloff after the new Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh positioned himself as a credible figure to fight inflation.

item.title: Indonesia Taps Insider to Revive World’s Worst Stock Market
item.description: Indonesia named capital markets veteran Jeffrey Hendrik as chief executive of its stock exchange, the latest effort to bolster investor confidence in the world’s worst-performing equity market.

item.title: Swiss AI Startup Prem Is Raising $100 Million Series A Round
item.description: Prem AI, a Swiss startup that helps companies including hedge funds and law firms run artificial intelligence models on their own private infrastructure, is raising $100 million in a Series A funding round.

item.title: Bond Traders Get a Clear Message From New Fed Chief on Inflation
item.description: Warsh says central bank won’t tolerate high inflation.

item.title: Europe Wants AI in Manufacturing Before Its Workforce Retires
item.description: As factory workers retire, Europe’s industries turn to AI to fill the coming labor gap.

item.title: Qatar Brings Empty LNG Ship Back for First Time Since War Began
item.description: Qatar brought an empty liquefied natural gas tanker back into the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz for the first time since the war in Iran began, a sign that the key producer is preparing to ramp up exports.

item.title: East Asian Refiners Eye Fuel Sales Boost Before Hormuz Reopen
item.description: Refineries in East Asia are starting to ramp up fuel exports after months of holding back and prioritizing local sales, in what appears to be an attempt to front-run the market before the Strait of Hormuz reopens.

item.title: Intel Shares Soar After Trump Says It Struck Apple Chip Deal
item.description: Intel Corp.’s stock surged more than 9% in premarket trading Thursday after US President Donald Trump said the chipmaker will work alongside Apple Inc. to design and produce semiconductors domestically.

item.title: EU Needs to Mandate Envoy for Ukraine Peace Talks, Latvia Says
item.description: Europe needs to appoint an envoy for peace talks with Russia to end the war in Ukraine when the time is right, Latvian Prime Minister Andris Kulbergs said.

item.title: EQT Buys UK’s Intertek for £9.3 Billion in Latest LSE Exit
item.description: Intertek Group Plc agreed to a £9.3 billion ($12.3 billion) takeover bid from private equity firm EQT AB, a deal that will mean another British company leaves the London stock market.

item.title: Atomic Lab Sounds Alarm on Screwworm Spread Across the Americas
item.description: A key international nuclear lab helping to eradicate the New World screwworm says more sterile flies and better coordination with southern neighbors is needed to prevent the deadly parasite from spreading across North America.

item.title: Hong Kong to Launch China Bond Futures to Promote Yuan
item.description: Hong Kong is set to launch China government bond futures amid Beijing’s efforts to promote global yuan usage and attract foreign capital.

item.title: SpaceX Shares Waver After Posting First Decline Since IPO
item.description: SpaceX shares fluctuated in premarket trading following the previous session’s drop, as it wraps up its first week as a public company following a record-breaking listing.

item.title: AI Company Dream Triples Value to $3 Billion in Funding Round
item.description: Dream, an Israeli artificial intelligence company that provides AI and cybersecurity services to governments and critical infrastructure operators, has raised $260 million at a $3 billion valuation.

item.title: Citi Sees $80 Billion Flows to India After RBI Capital Push
item.description: India could attract as much as $80 billion in foreign capital by year-end after the central bank unveiled measures to make the economy more attractive to overseas citizens and investors, according to Citigroup Inc.’s top banker in the country.

item.title: JPMorgan Warns Chip Stock Rally Poses Risk of Market ‘Tantrums’
item.description: The risk of market “tantrums” is rising as sharp swings in semiconductor stocks force some investors to cut allocations, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co. strategists.

item.title: Norway Holds Rate With Signal for Another Hike This Year
item.description: Norway’s central bank kept borrowing costs steady but sought to prepare investors for more tightening to calm some of the strongest price pressures in the region.

item.title: Jeremy Grantham on How to Tell If a Bubble Is About to Burst
item.description: From SpaceX to the South Sea.

item.title: Odd Lots: Jeremy Grantham on Spotting a Bubble’s End (Podcast)
item.description: Jeremy Grantham, co-founder and long-term strategist of GMO, has a long history of calling bubbles. As he recounts in his new memoir, The Making of a Permabear: The Perils of Long-Term Investing in a Short-Term World, that includes spotting the dot-com bubble of the early 2000s, which some people see as analogous to the current excitement over AI. And when it comes to today’s market, there are a lot of signs of frothiness you could point to. In this episode, we speak to Grantham about how he see

item.title: ECB’s Kocher Says Inflation to Stay for a While After Iran Deal
item.description: Consumer prices will stay higher for some time in the euro area despite an agreement to end the war in the Middle East, according to European Central Bank Governing Council member Martin Kocher.

item.title: US Gasoline Prices Fall Below $4 a Gallon as Iran Strain Eases
item.description: US gasoline prices dipped below $4 a gallon for the first time since March, providing welcome relief to consumers after a historic global supply disruption sent fuel costs soaring for months.

item.title: SNB Keeps Up Franc Intervention Threat With Rate Held at Zero
item.description: The Swiss National Bank retained its heightened readiness to sell the franc, guarding against renewed geopolitical turmoil in a decision that also left borrowing costs unchanged.

item.title: BHP Raises Potash Mine Costs Again, Takes $2.3 Billion Writedown
item.description: BHP Group said it expects the expansion of its giant Jansen potash mine to cost $6.9 billion, the latest in a series of cost and time overruns for the project in Canada.
